title: Phalangium coarctatum
taxonomy: Phalangium coarctatum is classified as a species of harvestman in the family Sclerosomatidae. It belongs to the order Opiliones, which is a group of arachnids that are often referred to as harvestmen or daddy long legs. The order Opiliones is further divided into several suborders, with Sclerosomatidae being a part of the suborder Eupnoi. Harvestmen are known for their eight long, thin legs and are often found in a variety of habitats, including forests, grasslands, and even urban areas. Overall, Phalangium coarctatum's classification reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the larger group of harvestmen.
